behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure boundary is between PR-change discovery and file analysis selection. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: this behavior depends on real PR diff/file-state behavior; a fake changed-file list can mask bugs in real diff collection or path resolution. Rejection-gate: I considered Integration, but rejected it because the behavior explicitly hinges on "files changed in the pull request," which is the real environment boundary that can fail outside in-process wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real git repo fixture with base and head commits, where an unchanged file contains a known lint violation and a changed file contains another. Run the lint step entrypoint as a real process using actual git diff selection. Assert only findings from changed files appear and unchanged-file violations are absent.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is primarily deterministic grouping/formatting logic. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess behavior is required to verify correct severity bucketing. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model decision or agent pipeline. Rejection-gate: I considered Integration because of "posted PR comment," but rejected it based on the concrete behavior text "grouped by severity (error, warning, info)," which narrows the failure to local transformation logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call the severity-grouping/comment-render function with mixed findings and assert output contains correctly grouped error/warning/info sections with expected membership.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is explicitly a runtime/timeout behavior in GitHub Actions, involving process execution time and environment constraints. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timing or in-process fakes can pass while real execution exceeds CI timeout. Rejection-gate: I considered Integration, but rejected it because "within the 10-minute Actions timeout" is a real system-runtime boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Generate a repo fixture with 200 changed files, run the lint step as an actual process in CI-like conditions, measure wall-clock completion, and assert completion and result publication occur within 10 minutes.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: the behavior explicitly names "linter process crashes," which is subprocess/exit/failure handling. MOCK-HIDE: exception stubs can miss real crash modes (exit signals, partial output, stream handling). Rejection-gate: I considered Integration, but rejected it because the concrete text "linter process crashes" requires real subprocess failure semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixture where one file triggers an actual linter process crash/fatal exit and other files contain known violations. Run the full step process and assert it continues processing remaining files and publishes aggregated results.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is local deduplication policy logic over findings data. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model output dependency or multi-agent sequencing. Rejection-gate: I considered Integration due "overlapping rules," but rejected it because the behavior target is the deterministic output property "deduplicated in the output," which is verifiable at function/object scope.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the deduplication component a findings set containing same-file/same-line duplicates from different overlapping rules and assert exactly one output entry per dedup key.
